subject: Physics
title: You Can't Know Both
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on You Can't Know Both.

hook.text: Try to pin down an electron's exact position and its speed becomes completely uncertain.

buildup.text: To see something, you hit it with light. But photons kick tiny particles, changing their momentum.

discovery.text: Use shorter wavelengths for precision and the photons carry more energy, disturbing the particle more.

twist.text: This isn't just a measurement problem. The particle literally doesn't have definite values for both.

climax.text: Uncertainty is fundamental to nature. It explains why atoms are stable and chemistry works.

punchline.text: Nature says: pick one. You can't have both.

quiz.question: Why is quantum uncertainty NOT just a measurement limitation?
options: Particles genuinely don't have definite position and momentum simultaneously
Our instruments aren't precise enough yet
